intro.title: try except
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Wrap risky code in try; catch exceptions with except ExceptionType:.

Prevents crash and lets you recover or inform the user.

Only catch exceptions you can handle meaningfully.

challenges.title: Easy: catch ValueError
Easy: try block
Medium: as e
Medium: recover
Hard: multiple lines try
Hard: no bare except
Expert: else try
Expert: raise after log
Expert: exception message
Expert: EAFP style

questions.prompt: Expert: else on try runs if:

intro.title: Multiple except blocks
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Order except clauses from specific to general. Subclass hierarchy matters.

Catch different types with separate handlers.

Too broad Exception catch hides bugs — use carefully.

challenges.title: Easy: KeyError
Easy: two handlers
Medium: order specific
Medium: tuple except
Hard: subclass
Hard: different messages
Expert: Exception last

validation.setupCode: ki=issubclass(KeyboardInterrupt, BaseException) and not issubclass(KeyboardInterrupt, Exception)

tests.label: Checks ki
tests.assertion: issubclass(KeyboardInterrupt, BaseException) and not issubclass(KeyboardInterrupt, Exception)

validation.setupCode: se=issubclass(SystemExit, BaseException) and not issubclass(SystemExit, Exception)

tests.label: Checks se
tests.assertion: issubclass(SystemExit, BaseException) and not issubclass(SystemExit, Exception)

challenges.title: Expert: log and rethrow

intro.title: else and finally
intro.bodyMarkdown:
try/except/else: else runs if no exception. finally always runs — cleanup.

Use finally for resources if not using with (rare).

else avoids putting success-only code in try block.

challenges.title: Easy: finally runs
challenges.starterCode:
try:
    pass
finally:
    done=True

challenges.title: Easy: else no error
Medium: finally with return
challenges.starterCode:
def demo():
 global ran
 try:
  return 1
 finally:
  ran=True
result=demo()

challenges.title: Medium: else success path
Hard: cleanup flag
challenges.starterCode:
try:
 pass
finally:
 c=True

challenges.title: Hard: no else on bare except
Expert: return in finally
challenges.starterCode:
def f():
 global ran
 try:
  return 1
 finally:
  ran=True
f()

challenges.title: Expert: suppress in except
Expert: else vs try body
Expert: with replaces finally
challenges.starterCode:
import io
data=""
f=io.StringIO("ok")
with f:
 data=f.read()

questions.prompt: Expert: return in try still runs:
Expert: exception in else caught by:

intro.title: Raising exceptions
intro.bodyMarkdown:
`raise ValueError("msg")` signals errors. Re-raise with bare `raise`.

Validate inputs and fail fast with clear messages.

Do not use exceptions for normal control flow casually.

challenges.title: Easy: raise ValueError
challenges.starterCode: raise ValueError("bad")

challenges.title: Easy: message
challenges.starterCode: raise RuntimeError("oops")

challenges.title: Medium: validate
Medium: reraise
Hard: raise from
Hard: TypeError
Expert: custom later
Expert: fail fast
Expert: not for flow
Expert: document raises

intro.title: Custom exceptions
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Subclass Exception: `class AppError(Exception): pass`.

Domain-specific errors improve clarity. Add attributes for context.

Keep hierarchy shallow; document when raised.

challenges.title: Easy: define class
challenges.starterCode:
class MyError(Exception):
    pass

challenges.title: Easy: raise custom
challenges.starterCode:
class E(Exception): pass
raise E()

challenges.title: Medium: inherit Exception
challenges.starterCode:
class E(Exception): pass
ok=issubclass(E,Exception)

challenges.title: Hard: catch custom
Hard: multiple custom
Expert: extra fields
Expert: HTTP style
Expert: document
challenges.starterCode:
class ScoreError(Exception):
 """Raised when score is invalid."""
 pass
doc=ScoreError.__doc__

tests.label: Checks doc
tests.assertion: doc is not None and "invalid" in doc.lower()

challenges.title: Expert: shallow tree
challenges.starterCode:
class AppError(Exception): pass
class LoginError(AppError): pass
shallow=issubclass(LoginError, AppError) and issubclass(AppError, Exception)

intro.title: Exception hierarchy
intro.bodyMarkdown:
BaseException → Exception → many types (ValueError, TypeError, OSError...).

Catch parent to handle group. KeyboardInterrupt inherits BaseException not Exception.

Read traceback bottom-up to find root cause.

challenges.title: Easy: ZeroDivisionError
Medium: catch ArithmeticError
Medium: traceback concept

tests.label: Checks order
tests.assertion: ValueError in order and Exception in order

challenges.title: Expert: root cause

intro.title: Assertions
intro.bodyMarkdown:
`assert condition, message` — AssertionError if false. For developer checks, not user validation.

python -O removes asserts. Use exceptions for user-facing errors.

Do not assert side effects or IO.
intro.sandboxCode:
def split_even(total, parts):
    assert parts > 0, "parts must be positive"
    return total / parts
print(split_even(10, 2))

challenges.title: Easy: assert message
challenges.starterCode: assert 1==1, "ok"

challenges.title: Medium: AssertionError
Medium: debug check
challenges.starterCode:
def f(x):
 assert x>=0
 return x
ok=f(1)==1

challenges.title: Hard: not for user input

intro.title: Debugging strategies
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Read traceback, print/log state, reproduce minimally, use debugger (pdb/breakpoint).

Rubber duck debugging — explain code line by line.

Fix root cause not symptoms; add test to prevent regression.

challenges.title: Easy: print debug
challenges.starterCode:
x=1
print("x=",x)

challenges.title: Medium: minimal repro
Hard: logging debug
challenges.starterCode:
import logging
order=logging.DEBUG < logging.INFO

challenges.title: Expert: regression test
Expert: binary search commits
Expert: type hints help

questions.prompt: logging better than print in prod:

intro.title: Logging basics
intro.bodyMarkdown:
logging module: debug, info, warning, error, critical levels.

Better than print for apps — timestamps, levels, handlers.

Configure once at startup; use logger = logging.getLogger(__name__).
intro.sandboxCode:
import logging
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
log.info("App started")
log.warning("Low disk")

challenges.title: Expert: exc_info True
challenges.starterCode:
import logging
import io
logged_tb=False
buf=io.StringIO()
log=logging.getLogger("t_exc")
log.handlers.clear()
h=logging.StreamHandler(buf)
log.addHandler(h)
log.setLevel(logging.ERROR)
try:
 int("x")
except ValueError:
 log.error("fail", exc_info=True)
logged_tb="Traceback" in buf.getvalue()

intro.title: Defensive programming
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Validate inputs, handle edge cases, use defaults, fail with clear errors.

Assume less; document preconditions; write tests for boundaries.

Balance defensiveness with readability — not every line needs a guard.

challenges.title: Easy: empty check
Medium: bounds
challenges.starterCode:
def in_bounds(i,n): return 0<=i<n
ok=in_bounds(5,3)

challenges.title: Medium: default arg None
challenges.starterCode:
def add_item(x,lst=None):
 if lst is None: lst=[]
 lst.append(x)
 return lst
a=add_item(1)
b=add_item(2)
shared=a is b

challenges.title: Hard: validate range
Hard: guard clause
challenges.starterCode:
def f(x):
 if x is None: return None
 return x*2
r=f(None)

Expert: test boundaries

levels.title: Easy project: CLI guess the number

intro.title: Easy project: CLI guess the number
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Build a number-guessing game in the terminal. Pick a secret, loop until the player guesses correctly, give higher/lower hints.

Use `random.randint`, a while loop, and int conversion with try/except for bad input.

Structure with a `main()` function and `if __name__ == "__main__"` guard.

challenges.title: Medium: while loop
challenges.starterCode:
while guess!=secret:
    guess=7

challenges.title: Medium: higher lower
Hard: attempt counter
challenges.starterCode: for _ in range(3): a+=1

challenges.title: Hard: main function

intro.title: Medium project: Quiz app
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Questions as list of dicts with prompt, choices, answer index. Score correct answers.

Shuffle questions or choices with random.shuffle. Show final percentage.

Time optional; store high score in file.
intro.sandboxCode:
questions = [
    {"q": "2+2?", "choices": ["3","4","5"], "a": 1},
]

def grade(answers):
    score = sum(1 for i, ans in enumerate(answers) if ans == questions[i]["a"])
    return score

print(grade([1]))

validation.setupCode:
questions=[{"a":1},{"a":0}]
answers=[1,0]
score=sum(1 for i,a in enumerate(answers) if a==questions[i]["a"]

challenges.title: Hard: shuffle
challenges.starterCode:
import random
lst=[1,2,3]
random.shuffle(lst)
same_set=sorted(lst)==[1,2,3]

intro.title: Hard project: Expense tracker
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Record expenses: amount, category, date, note. List by month; sum by category.

Use datetime for dates; store list, persist JSON.

Report: total, average, top category.
intro.sandboxCode:
from datetime import datetime

expenses = []

def add_expense(amount, category, note=""):
    expenses.append({
        "amount": amount,
        "category": category,
        "date": datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d"),
        "note": note,
    })

add_expense(12.5, "food", "lunch")
print(sum(e["amount"] for e in expenses))

challenges.title: Medium: strftime date
challenges.starterCode:
from datetime import datetime
d=datetime(2024,6,1).strftime("%Y-%m-%d")

f=[e for e in exp if e["category"]=="food"]
f=len(f)
